causal_chain.from_node: March 2020 earthquake in Utah caused A4's architect to be out of communication for two days, impacting project workflow.
causal_chain.to_node: Court found Modification A00001 acted as accord and satisfaction for earthquake delay claims, releasing government from further liability for March 2020 earthquake impacts.

causal_chain.from_node: March 2020 earthquake in Utah caused A4's architect to be out of communication for two days, impacting project workflow.
causal_chain.to_node: Government moved for summary judgment on HPTC contract claims for earthquake and COVID-19 impacts during March-August 2020, arguing Modification A00002 acted as accord and satisfaction.

causal_chain.from_node: Mississippi River levels exceeded 11 feet at Carrolton gauge for 230 days in 2019 and 171 days in 2020, preventing levee construction work per contract restrictions.
causal_chain.to_node: JAFEC subcontractor was unable to perform Deep Soil Mixing work during extended high river periods due to contract work restrictions.

causal_chain.from_node: JAFEC subcontractor was unable to perform Deep Soil Mixing work during extended high river periods due to contract work restrictions.

causal_chain.from_node: Mississippi River levels exceeded 11 feet at Carrolton gauge for 230 days in 2019 and 171 days in 2020, preventing levee construction work per contract restrictions.
causal_chain.to_node: Phylway submitted multiple requests for equitable adjustment for costs due to high river shutdowns, but the government denied compensation each time, maintaining only non-compensable time extensions were allowed under contract terms.

causal_chain.from_node: Mississippi River levels exceeded 11 feet at Carrolton gauge for 230 days in 2019 and 171 days in 2020, preventing levee construction work per contract restrictions.

precedents.citation: BCI Construction USA, Inc., Armed Services Board of Contract Appeals (2024)

causal_chain.from_node: BCI alleged that existing riprap exceeded the monolith joint where concrete removal/placement was required, creating a differing site condition that prevented completion of contract work without removal.
causal_chain.to_node: Contract included liquidated damages clause requiring $860 per day for delays in completion, with government calculating rate based on oversight staff costs and concurrent project assumptions.

causal_chain.from_node: Contract included liquidated damages clause requiring $860 per day for delays in completion, with government calculating rate based on oversight staff costs and concurrent project assumptions.
causal_chain.to_node: Government assessed liquidated damages at $860 per day for missed completion date, with BCI challenging the reasonableness of the rate calculation methodology.

causal_chain.from_node: Government issued revised drawings requiring installation of thickened slab for sidewalks via Modification No. A00013/R00020, altering the original contract requirements.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI requested government confirmation of sidewalk design via RFI-0055, stating the design lacked relief for concrete contraction and proposing joint details.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI requested government confirmation of sidewalk design via RFI-0055, stating the design lacked relief for concrete contraction and proposing joint details.
causal_chain.to_node: Government issued unilateral Modification No. R00026 awarding BCI $24,559.11 for installation of sidewalk isolation joints and sealant, adding 7 days to contract completion date.

causal_chain.from_node: Government issued revised drawings requiring installation of thickened slab for sidewalks via Modification No. A00013/R00020, altering the original contract requirements.
causal_chain.to_node: Government issued unilateral Modification No. R00026 awarding BCI $24,559.11 for installation of sidewalk isolation joints and sealant, adding 7 days to contract completion date.

causal_chain.from_node: Winter drawdown of Tuttle Creek Lake caused by weather conditions, inflows, and temperatures, resulting in excusable but noncompensable delays per FAR 52.249-10.
causal_chain.to_node: Government announced winter drawdown of Tuttle Creek Lake starting November 25, 2020, with 3000 cfs releases for approximately 10 days, acknowledging this would impact critical path activities.

causal_chain.from_node: Government announced winter drawdown of Tuttle Creek Lake starting November 25, 2020, with 3000 cfs releases for approximately 10 days, acknowledging this would impact critical path activities.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI responded that winter drawdown would impact all available work, inundate existing excavations, and require suspension of onsite activities until drawdown completion.

causal_chain.from_node: Government assessed liquidated damages from April 23, 2021 (BCI's claimed substantial completion date) until July 15, 2021 (actual acceptance date), which BCI disputes as improper after substantial completion.
causal_chain.to_node: Government formally notified BCI of liquidated damages assessment at $860 per day beginning July 12, 2020, due to missed July 11, 2020 completion date.

causal_chain.from_node: Government formally notified BCI of liquidated damages assessment at $860 per day beginning July 12, 2020, due to missed July 11, 2020 completion date.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI challenged government's liquidated damages assessment as unwarranted and without justifiable cause, disputing the validity of the damages.

causal_chain.from_node: Water seepage from a buried electrical line outside construction limits caused excessive water entering the project site's northwest side, which BCI claimed as a differing site condition.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI experienced excessive seepage of water into the project site from a hillside outside construction limits, caused by a buried electric line installed by another contractor in 2016.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI experienced excessive seepage of water into the project site from a hillside outside construction limits, caused by a buried electric line installed by another contractor in 2016.
causal_chain.to_node: Water began flowing from the electrical seep on April 27, 2019, intermittently seeping heavily into the project's northwest corner and impacting work, requiring container movement.

causal_chain.from_node: Water seepage from a buried electrical line outside construction limits caused excessive water entering the project site's northwest side, which BCI claimed as a differing site condition.
causal_chain.to_node: Water began flowing from the electrical seep on April 27, 2019, intermittently seeping heavily into the project's northwest corner and impacting work, requiring container movement.

causal_chain.from_node: Water seepage from a buried electrical line outside construction limits caused excessive water entering the project site's northwest side, which BCI claimed as a differing site condition.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI alleges the electrical seep constituted a Type II differing site condition where water entering the work site from outside construction limits differed materially from conditions indicated in contract documents.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI alleges the electrical seep constituted a Type II differing site condition where water entering the work site from outside construction limits differed materially from conditions indicated in contract documents.
causal_chain.to_node: Government alleges BCI failed to provide proper written notice of the differing site condition as required by FAR 52.236-2 before disturbing conditions, potentially prejudicing investigation.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI alleges the electrical seep constituted a Type II differing site condition where water entering the work site from outside construction limits differed materially from conditions indicated in contract documents.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI seeks recovery under superior knowledge doctrine, alleging government knew about wet conditions at electrical seep site but failed to disclose vital information affecting performance costs/duration.

causal_chain.from_node: Government issued a Stop Work Order due to impending high water releases from Tuttle Creek Lake, requiring backfilling and evacuation to prevent flooding.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI seeks recovery of COVID-19 related costs under the theory that government-caused delays pushed work into the pandemic period, while the government argues COVID-19 was unforeseeable and not compensable under the fixed-price contract.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI seeks recovery of COVID-19 related costs under the theory that government-caused delays pushed work into the pandemic period, while the government argues COVID-19 was unforeseeable and not compensable under the fixed-price contract.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI alleged 59-day delay in delivery of manhole covers from Oldcastle Infrastructure due to COVID-19 production issues impacting critical path activities.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI seeks recovery of COVID-19 related costs under the theory that government-caused delays pushed work into the pandemic period, while the government argues COVID-19 was unforeseeable and not compensable under the fixed-price contract.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI sought COVID-19 related time extensions and costs for subcontractor Nicholson, alleging pandemic impacts including travel restrictions and potential work suspension.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI sought COVID-19 related time extensions and costs for subcontractor Nicholson, alleging pandemic impacts including travel restrictions and potential work suspension.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI alleged 59-day delay in delivery of manhole covers from Oldcastle Infrastructure due to COVID-19 production issues impacting critical path activities.

causal_chain.from_node: Government issued a Stop Work Order due to impending high water releases from Tuttle Creek Lake, requiring backfilling and evacuation to prevent flooding.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI alleged the Stop Work Order resulted from a differing site condition, but government acknowledged it as a change entitling adjustment, not a DSC.

causal_chain.from_node: Government disapproved BCI's concrete mix design using Class F Fly Ash on April 26, 2018, stating it did not meet alkali-silica reactivity mitigation requirements with calcium oxide content exceeding 8% and total equivalent alkali content over 1.5%.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI submitted RFI-0021 on May 7, 2018, requesting approval of their preferred fly ash, but government responded on May 9, 2018, rejecting the request and suggesting alternative materials.

causal_chain.from_node: Government disapproved BCI's concrete mix design using Class F Fly Ash on April 26, 2018, stating it did not meet alkali-silica reactivity mitigation requirements with calcium oxide content exceeding 8% and total equivalent alkali content over 1.5%.
causal_chain.to_node: MCM stated they could not substitute alternative materials for fly ash due to plant limitations serving multiple customers and limited silo space, making material substitution impractical.

causal_chain.to_node: Court found Modification A00001 acted as accord and satisfaction for earthquake delay claims, releasing government from further liability for March 2020 earthquake impacts.

causal_chain.from_node: March 2020 earthquake in Utah caused A4's architect to be out of communication for two days, impacting project workflow.
causal_chain.to_node: Government moved for summary judgment on HPTC contract claims for earthquake and COVID-19 impacts during March-August 2020, arguing Modification A00002 acted as accord and satisfaction.

precedents.court: Armed Services Board of Contract Appeals
precedents.citation: BCI Construction USA, Inc., Armed Services Board of Contract Appeals (2024)

causal_chain.from_node: BCI alleged that existing riprap exceeded the monolith joint where concrete removal/placement was required, creating a differing site condition that prevented completion of contract work without removal.
causal_chain.to_node: Contract included liquidated damages clause requiring $860 per day for delays in completion, with government calculating rate based on oversight staff costs and concurrent project assumptions.

causal_chain.from_node: Contract included liquidated damages clause requiring $860 per day for delays in completion, with government calculating rate based on oversight staff costs and concurrent project assumptions.
causal_chain.to_node: Government assessed liquidated damages at $860 per day for missed completion date, with BCI challenging the reasonableness of the rate calculation methodology.

causal_chain.from_node: Government issued revised drawings requiring installation of thickened slab for sidewalks via Modification No. A00013/R00020, altering the original contract requirements.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI requested government confirmation of sidewalk design via RFI-0055, stating the design lacked relief for concrete contraction and proposing joint details.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI requested government confirmation of sidewalk design via RFI-0055, stating the design lacked relief for concrete contraction and proposing joint details.
causal_chain.to_node: Government issued unilateral Modification No. R00026 awarding BCI $24,559.11 for installation of sidewalk isolation joints and sealant, adding 7 days to contract completion date.

causal_chain.from_node: Government issued revised drawings requiring installation of thickened slab for sidewalks via Modification No. A00013/R00020, altering the original contract requirements.

causal_chain.from_node: Winter drawdown of Tuttle Creek Lake caused by weather conditions, inflows, and temperatures, resulting in excusable but noncompensable delays per FAR 52.249-10.
causal_chain.to_node: Government announced winter drawdown of Tuttle Creek Lake starting November 25, 2020, with 3000 cfs releases for approximately 10 days, acknowledging this would impact critical path activities.

causal_chain.from_node: Government announced winter drawdown of Tuttle Creek Lake starting November 25, 2020, with 3000 cfs releases for approximately 10 days, acknowledging this would impact critical path activities.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI responded that winter drawdown would impact all available work, inundate existing excavations, and require suspension of onsite activities until drawdown completion.

causal_chain.from_node: Government assessed liquidated damages from April 23, 2021 (BCI's claimed substantial completion date) until July 15, 2021 (actual acceptance date), which BCI disputes as improper after substantial completion.
causal_chain.to_node: Government formally notified BCI of liquidated damages assessment at $860 per day beginning July 12, 2020, due to missed July 11, 2020 completion date.

causal_chain.from_node: Government formally notified BCI of liquidated damages assessment at $860 per day beginning July 12, 2020, due to missed July 11, 2020 completion date.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI challenged government's liquidated damages assessment as unwarranted and without justifiable cause, disputing the validity of the damages.

causal_chain.from_node: Water seepage from a buried electrical line outside construction limits caused excessive water entering the project site's northwest side, which BCI claimed as a differing site condition.
causal_chain.to_node: BCI experienced excessive seepage of water into the project site from a hillside outside construction limits, caused by a buried electric line installed by another contractor in 2016.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI experienced excessive seepage of water into the project site from a hillside outside construction limits, caused by a buried electric line installed by another contractor in 2016.
causal_chain.to_node: Water began flowing from the electrical seep on April 27, 2019, intermittently seeping heavily into the project's northwest corner and impacting work, requiring container movement.

causal_chain.to_node: Government alleges BCI failed to provide proper written notice of the differing site condition as required by FAR 52.236-2 before disturbing conditions, potentially prejudicing investigation.

causal_chain.from_node: BCI alleges the electrical seep constituted a Type II differing site condition where water entering the work site from outside construction limits differed materially from conditions indicated in contract documents.
